title: cyathea aspera
intro: Cyathea aspera, also known as the rough tree fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. Cyathea aspera has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, and it has also been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and wellbeing.
